code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Negative updates erasing previous satisfaction
    - Premature failure (≤6 months) with functional impact
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification
    - Comparative inferiority without safety hazards

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Moderate praise using terms like "like"/"good" without enthusiasm
    - Mention of initial negatives requiring resolution (washing/break-in)
    - Decorative items meeting expectations without enthusiastic endorsement
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Material flaws impacting durability but not safety

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Use of enthusiastic modifiers ("love"/"excellent"/"perfect")
    - Overcomes initial skepticism with exceptional results
    - Decorative items fulfilling aesthetic purpose perfectly
    - 3+ functional aspects praised (utility/durability/design)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Close brand comparisons with daily-driver status
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite minor imperfections

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Love"/"excellent" →5 vs "like"/"good" →4
    - Resolved initial negatives →4 without explicit enthusiasm
    - Pure decorative perfection →5 / decorative+functional →4 with flaws
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Core function achieved post-setup →5 if flawless execution
    - Comparative praise →5 if "nearly matches" top brand with satisfaction
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Second purchase failed in 2 months. Dangerous!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Really like these potholders. Work well after initial wash removed plastic smell"
<Reasoning>Moderate praise with resolved negative →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Love how this cleaner handles puppy messes! Surpassed all expectations"
<Reasoning>Enthusiastic praise overcoming doubts →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Cute ornament completes our holiday decor perfectly"
<Reasoning>Pure decorative perfection →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Thin material worries me, but works for light organizing"
<Reasoning>Adequate with compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Constantly slides off counter despite good concept"
<Reasoning>Core functionality failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Does job but feels cheap compared to premium brands"
<Reasoning>Comparative inferiority →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Adore the aqua storage cube! Foldable but slightly small"
<Reasoning>Decorative+functional with minor flaw →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Non-stick coating works perfectly! Handle warmth irrelevant"
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with trivial issue →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Matches Lodge quality for daily use. Minor difference"
<Reasoning>Brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (34 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 15
  gold 4 -> 5: 10
  gold 5 -> 3: 2
  gold 2 -> 1: 1
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Made in the USA.  It's the only candles I'll buy since I've just read too much about what can be in foreign made candles.  Things like lead. (ugh)

Normally I buy Yankee candles but I found this Village Candle to be just as nice if not nicer.  The sc..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "When it comes to cookware cast iron is about as durable as it gets. Yes, it can be a bit harder to take care of (don't throw it in the dishwasher and towel dry it after washing) but it will literally last generations. This skillet came pre-seasoned w..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "This is a very pretty carpet runner and in real life it looks even better than the product photo.  The colors are rich and the pattern would go with a variety of design styles.

NOTES
==============
--this is a very flat carpet.
For my purposes in th..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I received this product for free for my honest opinion. I was not paid for my review.
The GhostBed Twin Knockdown Foundation wood mattress foundation is a great alternative to traditional boxsprings. This was easy to put together. It is also sturdy. ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This mattress get 4 stars for comfort, but there's so many other nice features about it that it gets 5 starts for everything else. To begin with, I wouldn't suggest using this as a long term every day bed for someone. The shallow design and budget pr..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I was actually looking for smaller pie cover; really only wanting something that would cover and keep my pumpkin pie from drying out when it was in the fridge.

For that purpose this Pie holder is overkill. It's LARGE. It's tall enough for meringues ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "On my computer screen the color for the 'cool brown' bath mat is accurate.  It's a faded sort of brown with lots of gray tint to it.  Our upstairs bath is in browns -- golden-hue brown and tile brown-- and it fits in.

What you get is a cool-brown no..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 34 errors, 17 borderline out of 80 examples."}
